transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hi, how are you?
Good, how are you?
I am good.
Is this your first day back at the office?
It is, we, in Israel, we work also Sunday, so yesterday was also a working day, but we work from home, basically.
Okay, okay.
I saw the internal link spreadsheet that you shared.
Yeah, thank you for putting that together.
I think this is really helpful.
I shared this with the content manager who is generating the content so that, in the content that we're working on this week and the following weeks, we will.
I'll take into account these links, we should be able to start implementing this right away.
It's also like...
It's not like a life and death situation, so we can use it smartly enough.
But it's supposed to give, like, a very good indication of where to link and what are the destinations and anchors we should choose.
Cool.
Thanks.
Hi, Libby.
Hi.
How's it going?
Good.
Let me just check to see if Nima is joining our call today.
Okay.
Looks like he rejected the invite.
How are you guys doing?
Is this your first day back?
are doing?
How are are How How How
It's my first day back.
Technically, were open on Friday, too, but I took that day off.
I don't know if, Rich, you were in there.
I was there on Friday along with three other people in the office, so, yeah.
I'm like, oh, please let this be the case all the time.
It'd be amazing.
That's cool.
I was out for most of last week as well, and whenever I'm out for a longer period of time, I either get sick or catch a cold, which is the case right now.
So I was like, it was a Saturday night.
was like, am I really getting sick right now?
This is just, it happened.
when you finally relax, you get a cold, yeah.
Yeah, yeah.
It really is quite something.
But, all right, I think we can get started.
So I've shared an agenda here with everyone.
So right as you guys were joining, Marana...
And I were discussing the internal links spreadsheet that he put together for us.
So it's basically just a list of landing pages from Google Search Console and then the queries or keywords that they rank for.
And we want to make sure that when we're linking to any of these pages, we're using one of these keywords as the anchor text to give these pages like a natural boost in search results.
So that, you know, also our internal linking strategy kind of leads to good SEO results.
There's a lot of URLs here, Moran.
So I don't know if you had, like, I think it's, how is it ordered by impressions or?
Yeah, it's ordered by impressions.
Okay.
You can play around.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay, cool.
Yeah.
But this is what we'll be using moving forward.

And just to confirm, so also moving forward, we will avoid linking to blog posts that are older than, I think, like a year, three months, like a year to six months, because you do have a lot of content on your site.
So we might sometimes, like the workflow might link to older pages, but I did instruct the content manager to delete anything that's super old.
Okay.
And then in addition to the links, I did put together some new artifacts.
But before we dive into that, I just wanted to see if there was anything top of mind that you guys wanted to discuss before we get into it.
Libby, did see you responded to the featured images that I shared.
Yeah, that was the only thing for me.
Besides just an update that, and I see it's on the agenda too, but we, or I'm meeting with our legal team later today to just make sure we have a good process in place for how these are reviewed.

So I'll get more details from them if they want to review everything or if we want to go through a process where certain things are flagged or how that should work so that we hopefully don't get bottlenecked.
Okay, cool.

Yeah, with the featured images, so whenever we have to use stock photography, we are limited to free stock photos, which aren't as, just there aren't as many options as they are when you are buying stock photography from like Shutterstock or other pages.
So that's why some of them feel a little, just the imagery isn't maybe as precisely what the brand guidelines specify.
I was
I mean, I can talk to Katya, our designer, to see if there was a way we could constrain or just kind of further adapt the image and workflow.
But other than that, I'm not, I'll have to like think about what's the best way we could do this so as not to like create a bottleneck with publishing once we start publishing.
Okay.
Yeah.
And I think let's give it one more try.
And if, if it still doesn't feel like it's going to work out, we can see if our design team can help come up with a solution as well.
Because we do have access to like Getty and some of those paid for images, but we pay per image too.
So, you know, obviously it's going to get pricey as we get moving.
But I do think we could try AI images.
They just, we just don't want them to look very obviously.
AI is the, the hard part.
And most of our image.
Images use people, so it's very easy to tell when a person is AI-generated still.
So it's kind of like a hard place to be, but maybe if we can give it one more try with your designer and then go from there.
But for the ones we have in process, we can use some of our images we have on file.
Okay, yeah, that makes sense.
I'll talk to Katya.
I mean, I think, I know you want to use humans, and yeah, getting humans right in an AI workflow is hard.

I don't know if it's a really, it's a hard rule to avoid illustrations or graphics, because when it comes to just pure illustrations or graphics or just like drawing type images, that's where the AI does a lot better.
And most of our clients actually use those types of images in their blog posts.
Yeah, we tend to use more photographs than...
Um, then animation, or sorry, illustration, um, I could, do you want me to run those ones by our design team that you sent?
Um, or what do you think?
I think it would be helpful, yeah, if you could just, like, share them and be like, what do you think?
Um, yeah, the other thing we tend to do too is use a photographic approach, but, like, cityscapes or travel environments rather than, like, the illustration.
We just don't have a, a illustration style, really, because we don't use it hardly at That makes sense.
Yeah.
Yeah, yeah.
Okay, I can tell Katia to prioritize, like, cityscapes and travel imagery for just a stock image selection.
I think that might help, um, but in the meantime, yeah, check with what they want.
yeah, and our, in that brand guidelines link, there's, like, a photo mood board, and you can see some of the cityscapes, or, like, we often use, like, highways, or,
So skyscrapers, whatever it might be, bridges.
So there's a lot of examples in there.
Okay.
Okay.
That makes sense.
All right.
We'll do that.
We actually have a client who recently, they experimented with removing the featured images completely from the blog page.
So they appear on the blog index page, but they don't appear at all on the blog page because the website's faster and they got better rankings.
So now it doesn't appear at all.
So it's just something I wanted to throw out there.
If you're thinking about improving page speed or anything like that, it's something that we see other companies doing.
And then we can have images.
I think this last batch of content that I sent, I added a screenshot that I found in, I think, a support section of your website just to illustrate how a specific feature works.
So we can still have those images, but yeah.
Okay.
That could be something good for us.
I saw Moran was nodding, so it sounds like you're not opposed to that.
It's something that I also considered at one point, like maybe removing and giving them and redesigning like the internal blog page to fit it.
That we would not really need like a hero image.
The only caveat is that, which is solvable, is when you share something on social and you want like a social meta image that would go along with a blog and would fit to the blog post somehow.
But I think it's manageable.
I don't know.
We can consider this and take this offline, Libby.
Okay.
Yeah.
Okay.
Cool.
All right.
And then to give you...
A quick content update.
So we are working on these five articles this week.
So you're gonna get them like in the next couple of days.
And like we mentioned, so there are four articles that are under legal review.
And there are five articles that I sent over, I think the week of Christmas, that are ready for the marketing team's review.
So you can find them.
If you go to Airtable, you can just find them.
In this view that I linked in the agenda.
I don't know why is Airtable being so slow today, but it's been, it's been a struggle getting it to open.
But yeah, those five are ready for your review as well.
Okay, and then to get into the artifacts.
So I made changes to this art.
And I added CTAs that link to the webinars and the guides that Rich shared two weeks ago.
So each CTA group includes demo CTAs, as well as watch the webinar, download the guide.
So they are included throughout.
And these, and our development team has already adjusted the article generation pipeline.
So the new content that we'll work on is going to take into account this new artifact.

And then for, again, I mean, I know this is a super, super long artifact, but I think it would be helpful if you took a look and just like pointed out anything that you think isn't a good like thing to emphasize or that we could replace maybe with.

And then I went in and I created a separate statistics artifact because Moran did highlight that we should be adding more statistics to content, and I was thinking, okay, what's an easy way of doing that without, like, doing just manual research every time.

So I got started by generating, like, creating this artifact using three reports that I found on your website, and I am in, right now I am in some kind of, like, sales qualification email chain, so I'm not getting emails because I downloaded these reports.
But all of the stats should be in this artifact right here, and I did ping our dev team last week to...
to add this artifact to the pipeline as well.
So this just allows us to programmatically add these statistics and also to surface key themes and takeaways from content, in the content.
I think we can also expand this with really like credible, like third party reports, but we just need to make sure that they're not sponsored by any of your competitors.
Yeah, that's great.
And this is, I can share this with the legal team too, because if they sign off on something like this, that might help the process.
Because if they know we're only using these Navan-owned statistics in the blogs, then they probably, there's less of a chance they feel like they need to review every single thing.
Yeah, yeah, totally.
I mean, it is like pull just from Navan's content.
So it should be good to go.
All right, great.
And let me see if there was anything else on the agenda.
Just the AI messaging.
I know that's still a work in progress.
But I think before we start working on any, like, AI-related content, I mean, I'm going to hold off on creating any AI-related content before we get this messaging, like, guidelines.
Yeah, let me follow up again on that.
I did before the break, but didn't get a response.
So I will try once more.
Okay, great.
Okay, I think those are all of the updates for now.
Now, like I said, we'll be in touch with the five new articles this week.
We'll
We are working on improving the writing guidelines to make them more, like slightly more conversational and like just include better transitions.
So, you know, feel free to continue calling out any sections where you feel like the transitions are a little bit choppy or the voice isn't on brand.
That's kind of any bad examples that you can highlight.
We really appreciate that because it helps us understand like what you don't want.
Got it.
Okay.
Great.
Any other questions or anything else that you guys wanted to discuss today?
I think that covers it, but I hope you feel better.
Thank you.
No, thanks.
I will drink some tea.
And yeah, this isn't a big meeting day because I feel like if it was a meeting day, would probably lose my voice by the end of the day.
So, yeah, but thank you so much for your time.
I will follow up with Katya on the images, and I'll keep you in the loop with what she says.
Okay.
Thank you so much.
Thank you.
See you later.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content progress, new artifacts, and image strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=170.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New SEO Strategy:** Internal links will use exact-match anchor text from a new GSC-based spreadsheet to boost target page rankings.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=173.0)
  - [**Image Strategy Pivot:** To resolve brand alignment issues with free stock photos, the team will prioritize cityscapes/travel imagery and explore removing hero images for page speed.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=292.0)
  - [**Content Automation:** Two new artifacts—one for CTAs, one for Navan-owned statistics—will be programmatically added to new articles, streamlining content creation and legal review.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=707.0)
  - [**Content Pipeline:** 5 articles are in progress, 5 await Navan marketing review in Airtable, and 4 are with legal.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=658.0)

## Topics

### Featured Image Strategy

  - [**Problem:** Free stock photos fail to meet brand guidelines, causing a bottleneck.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=326.0)
  - [**Solution:** Pivot to cityscapes/travel imagery (highways, skyscrapers) over people-focused shots.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=498.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This aligns with the brand's existing photo mood board and avoids the "obviously AI" look of AI-generated people.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=498.0)
  - [**Contingency:** Libby will consult Navan's design team for a solution if the pivot is unsuccessful.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=384.0)
  - [**Future Idea:** Remove hero images from blog posts to improve page speed.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=551.0)
      - [**Challenge:** Requires a new process for social media meta images.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=628.0)
      - [**Status:** To be discussed offline by Libby and Moran.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=609.0)

### Content Pipeline & Review

  - [**Legal Review Process:** Libby is meeting with legal today to define a clear, non-bottlenecking review process.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [**Content Status:**](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=658.0)
      - [**In Progress:** 5 articles (due this week).](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=658.0)
      - [**Awaiting Marketing Review:** 5 articles (in Airtable).](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=670.0)
      - [**Awaiting Legal Review:** 4 articles.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=670.0)
  - [**Writing Guidelines:** GrowthX is refining guidelines for a more conversational tone and smoother transitions. Navan's specific feedback on choppy sections is critical for this improvement.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=955.0)

### New Content Artifacts

  - [GrowthX developed two artifacts to automate content creation and improve quality.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=707.0)
  - [**1. CTA Artifact:**](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=707.0)
      - [**Purpose:** Programmatically adds CTAs for demos, webinars, and guides.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=707.0)
      - [**Status:** Integrated into the article generation pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=742.0)
      - [**Action:** Navan team to review the artifact's content for alignment.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=759.0)
  - [**2. Statistics Artifact:**](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=788.0)
      - [**Purpose:** Adds credible stats from Navan's own reports.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=788.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Using Navan-owned data streamlines legal review and provides a programmatic source for key themes.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=870.0)
      - [**Status:** Pending integration into the pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=831.0)
      - [**Action:** Libby to share with legal to potentially accelerate content approval.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=870.0)

### Internal Linking Strategy

  - [**Resource:** Moran created a spreadsheet of GSC landing pages and their ranking keywords.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=173.0)
  - [**New Rule:** Use these exact keywords as anchor text for internal links.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=173.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Provides a natural SEO boost to the linked pages.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=185.0)
  - [**New Rule:** Avoid linking to blog posts older than 12–18 months.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=247.0)

### AI Messaging Guidelines

  - [**Status:** Still pending from Navan.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=916.0)
  - [**Impact:** GrowthX will not create any AI-related content until these guidelines are provided.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=916.0)
  - [**Action:** Libby will follow up with the relevant team again.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=916.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Aida:**](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=1016.0)
      - [Instruct designer (Katya) to prioritize cityscapes/travel imagery.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=498.0)
      - [Follow up with Katya on the feasibility of adapting free stock photos.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=326.0)
  - [**Libby:**](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=870.0)
      - [Share the stats artifact with the legal team for review.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=870.0)
      - [Follow up on the AI messaging guidelines.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=916.0)
      - [Consult the Navan design team if the image strategy pivot is unsuccessful.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=384.0)
  - [**Libby & Moran:**](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=609.0)
      - [Discuss offline the feasibility of removing hero images from blog posts.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=609.0)
  - [**Navan Team:**](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=670.0)
      - [Review the 5 articles awaiting marketing review in Airtable.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=670.0)
      - [Review the new CTA artifact content for alignment.](https://fathom.video/share/j58QJs7XxyWsuxya5167JrwUXY3fc3ox?tab=summary&timestamp=759.0)

action_items.description: Share featured images w/ Navan Design for feedback
Brief Katya to prioritize cityscapes/travel imagery for featured images
Discuss offline w/ Libby removing featured images from blog post pages
Review CTA artifact; send feedback to Aida
Share statistics artifact w/ Legal; request sign-off
Follow up on AI messaging guidelines
